title: How to Concatenate Sequence Files
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3aap4","text":"Instructions for how to concatenate files on Windows PC using the command prompt.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Find the file in file explorer
Select 'Extract All' from the options and extract the files into the new destination

Set the directory to the folder that contains your unzipped files using the prompt: "cd C:\Users\YOURNAME\Desktop\YOURFILE" Make sure to include any spaces. The extracted file you need to set the directory to will most likely be within an additional file in the file that you designated the extraction to. You must specify the location even within the "desktop" location.
Use the "type" command to concatenate all files within the directory. Using this prompt: "type *fastq.gz > NEWFILENAME.fq"
You now should have a concatenated file of all of your sample files into one.
creator: Wesley Turner
